title: The Tourism Scissors: Analyzing the Impact of Economic Indicators on Domestic vs. International Passenger Traffic in Turkey’s Tourism Hubs
text:
I’m honored to be a member of the FlyNomics project team.
Below, you’ll find a brief summary of our project. To access a detailed project description, please go to emu-hacettepe-analytics/flynomics.
Summary
As part of my EMU 430 course, our team, “Flynomics,” decided to dive into a highly relevant and visible issue: the shifting dynamics of travel in Türkiye. We call our project the “Tourism Scissors” analysis.

What is the “Tourism Scissors”?
If you live in Türkiye, you might have noticed a recent trend: while coastal airports and hotels are packed with international tourists, local vacationers are becoming a rare sight. We wanted to see if the data actually backed up this everyday observation.
The “scissors” refers to the X-shaped divergence we found in our graphs. Especially after 2021, as international flight demand continued to surge, domestic flight demand sharply collapsed.


The Data Behind the Story
To keep our analysis grounded in reality, we couldn’t just look at passenger numbers; we needed to pair mobility data with macroeconomic indicators. We processed and visualized everything in R using dplyr and ggplot2.
Our main data sources were: * DHMI (State Airports Authority): We filtered monthly passenger traffic specifically for major holiday hubs (Antalya, Izmir, Dalaman, and Milas-Bodrum). * CBRT EVDS (Central Bank of Türkiye): We pulled the average monthly USD/TRY exchange rate and inflation (CPI) figures to represent economic pressure.


Our Key Findings
After cleaning the data and running our Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA), a few striking realities emerged:

The Peak Season Divergence: The “scissors” effect is most visible during the summer months. It clearly illustrates how domestic citizens are essentially priced out of high-season tourism.
The Exchange Rate Impact: There is a strong, direct relationship between the rising USD/TRY exchange rate and the drop in domestic travel volumes. While the depreciating Lira attracts foreign tourists, it acts as a massive barrier for locals.
Winter Travel is Resilient: Interestingly, winter passenger trends display a much more parallel trajectory. This suggests that baseline travel (like business or essential trips) is much less elastic and less affected by economic shocks compared to leisure travel.

This project was a great opportunity to apply data analytics to a real-world problem. It clearly showed us that behind every macroeconomic statistic, there is a measurable impact on human behavior and mobility.

Industrial Engineering Student
Junior Industrial Engineering student at Hacettepe University. Passionate about sustainability, innovation, and collaborative projects. Quick learner with strong analytical and communication skills.

Candidate Engineer in the Production Planning and Control department. Involved in aerospace engineering practices including aircraft systems, manufacturing processes, and engineering workflows while developing knowledge of aviation industry standards.

title: Assignment 1
text:
Veri Bilimi ve Endüstri Mühendisliği Üzerine Sohbetler - Baykal Hafızoğlu & Erdi Daşdemir



Mr. Baykal talked about his education and career journey and explained how optimization and analytics are used in business life. He introduced the concepts of descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive analytics and stated that industrial engineering courses provide a strong foundation for these areas. He emphasized that the most important step in analytics projects is correctly defining the problem, determining measurable KPIs, and turning the solution into a software or system that users can actually use. He also mentioned that students who want to specialize in optimization should improve their mathematical modeling skills, practice with tools such as Python, and gain experience by working on real-world problems.



Question: Why is it important to correctly define the problem and determine KPIs in analytics projects according to Mr. Baykal?

Answer: It is important because defining the problem correctly and determining KPIs makes it possible to measure whether the solution actually works and to show the improvement quantitatively.



Question: According to Mr. Baykal, why is it important to correctly define the problem and determine KPIs in analytics projects?

To make the project take longer
To measure the success of the solution and show improvement quantitatively
To write more code
Only to prepare reports

Correct Answer: B) To measure the success of the solution and show improvement quantitatively.
